cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The study of energy transformations, especially heat and work, in chemical and physical processes

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The total kinetic and potential energy of all particles in a system

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $\Delta U = q + w$ where $q$ is heat and $w$ is work

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A reaction that releases heat to the surroundings ($\Delta H < 0$)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A reaction that absorbs heat from the surroundings ($\Delta H > 0$)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The heat content of a system at constant pressure; $H = U + PV$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $\Delta H_{\text{rxn}}^\circ = \sum \Delta H_f^\circ(\text{products}) - \sum \Delta H_f^\circ(\text{reactants})$
cards.answer: Standard enthalpy of reaction (Hess's Law summation)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Hess's Law
cards.answer: The total enthalpy change for a reaction is the same regardless of the pathway taken; $\Delta H$ is additive over multiple steps

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The enthalpy change when one mole of a substance is formed from its elements in their standard states

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $q = mc\Delta T$ where $m$ = mass, $c$ = specific heat capacity, $\Delta T$ = temperature change

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The amount of heat needed to raise 1 g of water by 1°C

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A measure of the disorder or randomness of a system

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Second law of thermodynamics
cards.answer: The total entropy of the universe increases for any spontaneous process: $\Delta S_{\text{univ}} > 0$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Third law of thermodynamics
cards.answer: The entropy of a perfect crystal at absolute zero ($0$ K) is zero

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $\Delta G = \Delta H - T\Delta S$ at constant temperature and pressure

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Gibbs free energy ($\Delta G$)
cards.answer: The maximum useful work a system can do; if $\Delta G < 0$, the process is spontaneous

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Conditions under which $\Delta G$ is always negative (always spontaneous)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Conditions under which $\Delta G$ is always positive (never spontaneous)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: An experimental device used to measure heat flow in chemical reactions at constant pressure

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A calorimeter that measures heat at constant volume, used for combustion reactions

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $\Delta H_{\text{rxn}} = \sum D(\text{bonds broken}) - \sum D(\text{bonds formed})$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The enthalpy change when one mole of a substance undergoes complete combustion with $\text{O}_2$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: When a gas dissolves in a liquid, entropy generally...
cards.answer: Decreases, because gas molecules (high disorder) become constrained in solution (lower disorder)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $\Delta S_{\text{rxn}}^\circ = \sum S^\circ(\text{products}) - \sum S^\circ(\text{reactants})$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The standard conditions for thermodynamic measurements: 298 K, 1 atm, 1 M concentrations
